mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators who need to manage clusters across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of initial setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This repository contains the necessary charts for installation.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Cluster API to function.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains all the necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API controller
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API controller using Helm, which manages the lifecycle of Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> Ensure you are installing in the kube-system namespace.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource that defines the desired state of your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infra-cluster
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureProviderCluster' with your actual infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infra-cluster
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Cluster API components to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Look for the Cluster API controller pods and ensure they are in the Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this will delete all managed clusters and associated resources.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This command removes the Cluster API controller.
> This command removes the CRDs.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your cluster configurations. This step will upgrade the Cluster API controller to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o yaml > cluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Backup your current cluster configurations.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API controller.
> Verify that the upgraded pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the Cluster CRD is not installed or not recognized.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error usually indicates that the Cluster API controller is not able to communicate with the Kubernetes API server.

**Fix:**
Check the network policies and ensure that the Cluster API controller has the necessary permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster API controller pods are failing to start due to configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system and resolve any configuration errors.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: no available machines'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that the infrastructure provider is not able to provision machines.

**Fix:**
Verify the infrastructure provider configuration and ensure that the necessary resources are available.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved the stability of existing controllers.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API versions for infrastructure providers.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your custom resources to the new API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the Cluster API controller pods running in the kube-system namespace with the output: 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' followed by the pod names and their statuses.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infra-cluster
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster with the necessary tools installed.
